recipes.name: Cantucci ai Pistacchi, Cranberry e Mandorla con Glassa Bianca
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, and a pinch of salt.
In a separate bowl, lightly beat eggs with olive oil, vanilla extract, and almond extract.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients. Mix until just combined, then fold in chopped pistachios and dried cranberries until evenly distributed. The dough will be stiff.
Divide the dough in half and on the prepared baking sheet, form two logs, about 30cm (12 inches) long and 4cm (1.5 inches) wide. Leave space between them.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown and firm. Remove from oven and let cool for 10-15 minutes.
Reduce oven temperature to 150°C (300°F). Using a serrated knife, slice the logs diagonally into 1.5-2cm (1/2-3/4 inch) thick biscuits. Lay them cut-side down on the baking sheet.
Bake for another 15-20 minutes, flipping halfway, until dry and crisp. Let cool completely. Melt the white almond bark according to package directions and drizzle generously over the cooled cantucci.

recipes.name: Torta all'Olio d'Oliva con Pistacchio e Mandorla
ingredients: Zest of 1 lemon (optional, for authentic Italian flavor)
steps: Preheat oven to 175°C (350°F). Grease and flour a 20cm (8-inch) round springform pan.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, ground pistachios, baking powder, and lemon zest (if using).
In a separate bowl, whisk eggs and sugar until light and fluffy. Gradually drizzle in the olive oil, milk, vanilla extract, and almond extract, mixing until well combined.
Gently fold the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients in three additions, mixing until just combined. Do not overmix. Stir in the chopped dried cranberries.
Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top. Bake for 40-45 minutes, or until a wooden skewer inserted into the center comes out clean.
Remove from oven and let cool in the pan for 10 minutes before inverting onto a wire rack to cool completely.
Once cooled, melt the white almond bark. Drizzle the melted bark over the cake. Optionally, dust with powdered sugar before serving.

recipes.name: Amaretti Morbidi ai Pistacchi e Cranberry con Filo di Cioccolato Bianco
ingredients: 2 tbsp '00' flour (to help bind the fruit and nuts)
steps: Preheat oven to 160°C (325°F). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, combine almond flour, granulated sugar, and 2 tbsp '00' flour. Mix well.
In a separate clean bowl, whisk egg whites with a pinch of salt until soft peaks form.
Gently fold the egg whites into the dry ingredients, along with vanilla extract and almond extract, until just combined. Then fold in the finely chopped pistachios and dried cranberries.
Scoop small amounts of the dough (about 1 tablespoon each) onto the prepared baking sheet, leaving some space between them. You can use a small ice cream scoop or two spoons.
Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until lightly golden around the edges and set. They should still be soft in the center.
Remove from oven and let cool completely on the baking sheet before transferring to a wire rack.
Once cooled, melt the white almond bark according to package directions and drizzle artfully over the amaretti before serving.

recipes.name: Crostata Rustica ai Frutti Secchi (Pistacchio e Cranberry) con Sfoglie di Mandorla
ingredients: 50g white almond bark, melted (for drizzle and almond shards)
steps: To make the dough: In a large bowl, combine flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, and lemon zest. Make a well in the center. Add the egg, olive oil, and vanilla extract. Mix with a fork, then bring together with your hands to form a smooth dough. Wrap in plastic wrap and chill for 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Lightly grease and flour a 24cm (9.5-inch) tart pan.

For the filling: In a small saucepan, combine chopped pistachios, dried cranberries, sugar, and water/orange juice. Bring to a gentle simmer for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened. Let cool.

Divide the chilled dough into two pieces, one slightly larger than the other. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the larger piece of dough to fit the tart pan. Press into the pan, trimming any excess. Spread the cooled filling evenly over the base.

Roll out the remaining dough and cut into strips. Arrange the strips in a lattice pattern over the filling. Trim and press the ends to seal with the base dough. You can also make small leaf shapes or other decorations from the remaining dough.
Bake for 35-40 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown. If the edges brown too quickly, cover lightly with foil.

Remove from oven and let cool completely on a wire rack. Melt the white almond bark. Drizzle some over the crostata. For almond shards, spread a thin layer of melted bark on parchment paper, let it set slightly, then break into pieces to decorate the tart if desired.

recipes.name: Semifreddo al Pistacchio e Mandorla con Coulis di Cranberry
ingredients: 1/2 tsp extra virgin olive oil (for richness and gloss)
50g white almond bark, melted and chilled for shards
steps: Line a loaf pan (approx. 20x10cm) with plastic wrap, ensuring overlap on the sides for easy removal. Place a metal bowl and whisk attachment in the freezer for cream whipping.

In a heatproof bowl set over a saucepan of simmering water (bain-marie), whisk egg yolks and 70g of sugar constantly until pale, thick, and ribbony (sabayon). Remove from heat and stir in vanilla and almond extracts, and ground pistachios. Let cool completely.
In the chilled bowl, whip the cold heavy cream until soft peaks form. In another clean bowl, whisk egg whites until stiff peaks form, gradually adding the remaining 30g of sugar.
Gently fold the whipped cream into the cooled pistachio sabayon mixture until just combined. Then, carefully fold in the stiff egg whites in two additions, maintaining as much air as possible.
Pour the semifreddo mixture into the prepared loaf pan, smoothing the top. Cover tightly with the overlapping plastic wrap and freeze for at least 4-6 hours, or until firm.

While semifreddo freezes, prepare the cranberry coulis: In a small saucepan, combine dried cranberries, water, and sugar. Bring to a simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes until cranberries are soft and the liquid slightly thickens. Stir in the olive oil for a hint of richness and gloss. Let cool, then blend if a smoother coulis is desired.
For garnish: Melt white almond bark and spread thinly onto parchment paper. Chill until firm, then break into shards. Alternatively, drizzle melted bark over semifreddo just before serving.
To serve: Invert the semifreddo onto a serving platter. Remove plastic wrap. Slice the semifreddo and serve with a generous drizzle of the cranberry coulis and decorate with white almond bark shards.

recipes.name: Cantucci Toscani ai Pistacchi e Mirtilli Rossi
ingredients: 50g (1/2 cup) shelled pistachio nuts, roughly chopped
50g (1/4 cup) white almond bark, melted (for drizzling)
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate bowl, lightly beat the eggs with vanilla extract and almond extract.
Pour the egg mixture into the dry ingredients. Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Add chopped pistachios and dried cranberries, kneading lightly until just combined. Do not overmix.
Divide the dough into two equal portions. On the prepared baking sheet, shape each portion into a log about 25-30 cm (10-12 inches) long and 5 cm (2 inches) wide.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown and firm to the touch. Remove from oven and let cool slightly for 10-15 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 150°C (300°F).

Carefully transfer the logs to a cutting board. Using a serrated knife, slice the logs diagonally into 1.5-2 cm (1/2 to 3/4 inch) thick biscuits. Place the slices cut-side up back on the baking sheet.
Bake for another 15-20 minutes, flipping halfway through, until dry and crisp. Let cool completely. Once cool, drizzle with melted white almond bark.

recipes.name: Torta all'Olio d'Oliva al Limone con Pistacchi e Glassa alla Mandorla Bianca
ingredients: 50g (1/2 cup) shelled pistachio nuts, roughly chopped
steps: Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Grease and flour a 20cm (8-inch) round cake pan.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt. Add lemon zest and half of the chopped pistachios to the dry ingredients, mixing to combine.
In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs, olive oil, milk, and vanilla extract until well combined.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients. Mix with a spatula or whisk until just combined and no streaks of flour remain. Be careful not to overmix.
Pour the batter into the prepared cake pan and spread evenly.
Bake for 35-40 minutes, or until a wooden skewer inserted into the center comes out clean.
Remove from oven and let cool in the pan for 10 minutes before inverting onto a wire rack to cool completely.

Once cool, prepare the glaze: Melt white almond bark. Stir in milk (if needed for desired consistency) and almond extract. Drizzle the glaze over the cooled cake and sprinkle with the remaining chopped pistachios.

recipes.name: Pasticcini Siciliani al Pistacchio e Mandorla
ingredients: 150g (1 1/2 cups) almond flour (or blanched almonds, finely ground)
75g (3/4 cup) shelled pistachio nuts, finely ground (some left whole for decoration, optional)
Zest of 1/2 organic lemon (optional, for brightness)
50g (1/4 cup) white almond bark, melted (for drizzling/dipping, optional)
steps: Preheat oven to 160°C (325°F). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, combine almond flour, ground pistachios, granulated sugar, and salt. If using, add lemon zest.
In a separate clean bowl, whisk egg whites until frothy but not stiff peaks. Stir in vanilla extract and almond extract.
Add the egg white mixture to the dry ingredients. Mix with a spatula until a thick, sticky paste forms.

Using a piping bag with a star tip or two spoons, form small cookies (about 3-4 cm / 1.5 inches in diameter) on the prepared baking sheet. Leave some space between them. You can press a whole pistachio nut in the center of each cookie, if desired.
Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until lightly golden around the edges and slightly firm to the touch. They will still be soft when hot, firming up as they cool.
Let cool completely on the baking sheet before gently transferring them. Once cool, dust with powdered sugar (optional) or dip/drizzle with melted white almond bark.

recipes.name: Baci di Dama al Pistacchio con Cuore di Mandorla Bianca
ingredients: 100g (1 cup) finely ground shelled pistachio nuts
100g (1/2 cup) white almond bark, chopped or chips (for filling)
steps: In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, ground pistachios, sugar, and salt.
Add the softened butter, vanilla extract, and almond extract to the dry ingredients. Mix with your hands or a stand mixer with a paddle attachment until a cohesive dough forms. Do not overmix.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to firm up.
Preheat oven to 160°C (325°F). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Take small portions of dough (about 1 tsp each) and roll them into small balls, approximately 2 cm (3/4 inch) in diameter. Place them on the prepared baking sheet, leaving a small space between each.
Bake for 12-15 minutes, or until the edges are lightly golden. The cookies should still be pale on top. Let cool completely on a wire rack.

Melt the white almond bark using a double boiler or microwave. Once melted and smooth, take one cooled cookie, spoon a small amount of melted white almond bark onto its flat side, and gently press another cookie on top to form a 'kiss' or sandwich.
Repeat with the remaining cookies. Allow the white almond bark to set completely before serving.

recipes.name: Crostata di Frutta con Frolla Profumata al Pistacchio e Mirtillo Rosso
ingredients: 75g (5 tbsp) unsalted butter, cold and cubed
30g (1/4 cup) finely ground shelled pistachio nuts
200g (approx. 3/4 cup) good quality cranberry jam (or homemade: 100g dried cranberries, 50g sugar, 100ml water, simmer until thick)
steps: Prepare the Pasta Frolla: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, ground pistachios, sugar, baking powder, and salt. Add cold butter cubes and rub into the flour mixture with your fingertips until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Add the egg, vanilla extract, almond extract, and lemon zest (if using). Mix until a dough forms. Do not overwork. Form into a disc, wrap in plastic, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.

Assemble the Crostata: Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F). Lightly grease a 22-24cm (9-inch) tart pan. On a lightly floured surface, roll out about two-thirds of the chilled dough into a circle large enough to fit the tart pan. Gently press it into the pan, trimming any excess.

Spread the cranberry jam evenly over the pastry base. If using dried cranberries to make jam, combine them with sugar and water, then simmer until thick, mashing some cranberries as it cooks. Let cool slightly before spreading.
Roll out the remaining dough and cut into strips (or use cookie cutters for decorative shapes) to create a lattice top or decorative edge over the jam.
Bake for 35-40 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the jam is bubbling gently.
Remove from oven and let cool completely on a wire rack. If using, drizzle with melted white almond bark before serving.

recipes.name: Biscotti Croccanti ai Pistacchi, Mandorle e Mirtilli Rossi con Glassa Bianca
ingredients: 10g lievito in polvere (baking powder, approx 2 tsp)
1 cucchiaino estratto di vaniglia (1 tsp vanilla extract)
1/2 cucchiaino estratto di mandorla (1/2 tsp almond extract)
70g pistacchi sgusciati (shelled pistachio nuts), roughly chopped
2 cucchiai olio d'oliva extra vergine (2 tbsp olive oil)
100g cioccolato bianco per copertura (white almond bark, or white chocolate coating)
1 cucchiaio olio d'oliva extra vergine (1 tbsp olive oil) - for glaze
steps: Preriscaldare il forno a 180°C (350°F) e foderare una teglia con carta da forno.
In una ciotola grande, mescolare la farina, lo zucchero, il lievito e il sale.
In una ciotola separata, sbattere leggermente le uova con 2 cucchiai di olio d'oliva, l'estratto di vaniglia e l'estratto di mandorla.
Versare gli ingredienti liquidi negli ingredienti secchi e mescolare fino a quando non sono appena combinati. Incorporare i pistacchi e i mirtilli rossi. L'impasto sarà appiccicoso.
Dividere l'impasto a metà e, su una superficie leggermente infarinata, modellare ogni metà in un filone, lungo circa 25-30 cm e largo 5 cm. Trasferire i filoni sulla teglia preparata.
Cuocere per 25-30 minuti, o fino a quando non saranno dorati e sodi. Togliere dal forno e lasciare raffreddare per 10 minuti. Ridurre la temperatura del forno a 150°C (300°F).

Usando un coltello seghettato, tagliare i filoni in diagonale in fette spesse 1,5-2 cm. Disporre le fette tagliate sulla teglia e cuocere per altri 20-25 minuti, girando a metà cottura, fino a quando non saranno asciutte e croccanti. Lasciare raffreddare completamente.

Sciogliere il cioccolato bianco per copertura a bagnomaria o nel microonde. Incorporare 1 cucchiaio di olio d'oliva per renderlo più fluido. Intingere un'estremità di ogni biscotto nel cioccolato fuso o cospargere sopra. Lasciare rapprendere su carta da forno prima di servire.

recipes.name: Torta all'Olio d'Oliva e Pistacchio
ingredients: 100ml olio d'oliva extra vergine (extra virgin olive oil)
80g pistacchi sgusciati (shelled pistachio nuts), finemente tritati
10g lievito in polvere (baking powder, approx 2 tsp)
1 cucchiaino estratto di vaniglia (1 tsp vanilla extract)
Zucchero a velo (icing sugar) per spolverare (opzionale)
steps: Preriscaldare il forno a 175°C (350°F). Imburrare e infarinare una teglia rotonda da 20 cm.
In una ciotola grande, mescolare la farina, i pistacchi finemente tritati, il lievito e il sale.
In un'altra ciotola, sbattere le uova e lo zucchero fino a ottenere un composto chiaro e spumoso. Incorporare gradualmente l'olio d'oliva, poi il latte, l'estratto di vaniglia e la scorza di limone.
Incorporare delicatamente gli ingredienti liquidi negli ingredienti secchi fino a quando non sono appena combinati. Fare attenzione a non mescolare eccessivamente.
Versare l'impasto nella teglia preparata e livellare la superficie.
Cuocere per 40-45 minuti, o fino a quando uno stecchino inserito al centro non esce pulito.
Lasciare raffreddare la torta nella teglia per 10 minuti prima di capovolgerla su una gratella per farla raffreddare completamente.
Spolverare con zucchero a velo prima di servire, se lo si desidera.

recipes.name: Amaretti Morbidi al Profumo di Mandorla e Pistacchio
ingredients: 200g farina di mandorle (almond flour) o mandorle pelate e tritate finemente (finely ground blanched almonds)
50g pistacchi finemente tritati (finely ground pistachio nuts)
2 albumi d'uovo grandi (large egg whites), a temperatura ambiente (circa 70g)
1/2 cucchiaino estratto di mandorla (1/2 tsp almond extract)
1/2 cucchiaino estratto di vaniglia (1/2 tsp vanilla extract)
Zucchero a velo (icing sugar) per rivestire
steps: Preriscaldare il forno a 160°C (325°F). Foderare due teglie con carta da forno.
In una ciotola grande, unire la farina di mandorle, i pistacchi macinati, lo zucchero semolato e 1 cucchiaio di farina 00.
In una ciotola pulita separata, sbattere gli albumi con un pizzico di sale fino a ottenere picchi morbidi.

Incorporare delicatamente il composto di mandorle/pistacchi negli albumi, aggiungendo l'estratto di mandorla e l'estratto di vaniglia. Mescolare fino a quando non sono appena combinati, formando un impasto appiccicoso.
Mettere lo zucchero a velo in un piatto fondo. Prelevare piccole porzioni di impasto (circa 1 cucchiaio) e arrotolarle in palline.
Rotolare ogni pallina nello zucchero a velo per ricoprirle completamente, quindi posizionarle sulle teglie preparate, lasciando circa 2-3 cm di spazio tra di loro.
Cuocere per 18-22 minuti, o fino a quando non saranno leggermente dorati sui bordi e leggermente crepati in superficie. Dovrebbero rimanere morbidi al centro.
Lasciare raffreddare sulle teglie per qualche minuto prima di trasferirli su una gratella per farli raffreddare completamente. Si rassoderanno mentre si raffreddano.

recipes.name: Biscotti Morbidi all'Olio d'Oliva con Mirtilli Rossi e Limone
ingredients: 80ml olio d'oliva extra vergine (extra virgin olive oil)
10g lievito in polvere (baking powder, approx 2 tsp)
1 cucchiaino estratto di vaniglia (1 tsp vanilla extract)
Zucchero di canna (coarse sugar) per cospargere (opzionale)
steps: Preriscaldare il forno a 180°C (350°F). Foderare una teglia con carta da forno.
In una ciotola grande, mescolare la farina, lo zucchero, il lievito e il sale.
In una ciotola separata, sbattere le uova, l'olio d'oliva, l'estratto di vaniglia e la scorza di limone fino a quando non sono ben combinati.
Aggiungere gli ingredienti liquidi agli ingredienti secchi e mescolare fino a quando non si forma un impasto morbido. Incorporare i mirtilli rossi essiccati.
Su una superficie leggermente infarinata, arrotolare l'impasto in un tronco spesso, oppure formare piccole palline (circa 30g ciascuna) e appiattirle leggermente.
Disporre i biscotti sulla teglia preparata. Se lo si desidera, spennellare leggermente con il latte e cospargere con zucchero di canna.
Cuocere per 18-22 minuti, o fino a quando non saranno leggermente dorati.
Lasciare raffreddare sulla teglia per qualche minuto prima di trasferirli su una gratella per farli raffreddare completamente.

recipes.name: Quadrotti al Pistacchio e Mandorla con Glassa Bianca
ingredients: 80ml olio d'oliva extra vergine (extra virgin olive oil)
80g pistacchi finemente tritati (finely ground pistachio nuts)
1 cucchiaino estratto di mandorla (1 tsp almond extract)
1 cucchiaino estratto di vaniglia (1 tsp vanilla extract)
10g lievito in polvere (baking powder, approx 2 tsp)
50g cioccolato bianco per copertura (white almond bark, or white chocolate coating)
1 cucchiaio olio d'oliva extra vergine (1 tbsp olive oil) - for glaze
2 cucchiai mirtilli rossi essiccati (dried cranberries), tritati - per guarnire
1 cucchiaio pistacchi tritati (chopped pistachio nuts) - per guarnire
steps: Preriscaldare il forno a 175°C (350°F). Imburrare e infarinare una teglia quadrata da 20x20cm.
In una ciotola grande, mescolare la farina, i pistacchi macinati, il lievito e il sale.
In una ciotola separata, sbattere le uova e lo zucchero fino a ottenere un composto chiaro e spumoso. Incorporare l'olio d'oliva, l'estratto di mandorla e l'estratto di vaniglia.
Aggiungere gradualmente gli ingredienti liquidi agli ingredienti secchi, mescolando fino a quando non sono appena combinati. Non mescolare eccessivamente.
Versare l'impasto nella teglia preparata e stenderlo uniformemente.
Cuocere per 30-35 minuti, o fino a quando uno stecchino inserito al centro non esce pulito. Lasciare raffreddare completamente nella teglia su una gratella.

Una volta raffreddato, sciogliere il cioccolato bianco per copertura a bagnomaria o nel microonde. Incorporare 1 cucchiaio di olio d'oliva per renderlo leggermente più fluido per una glassa più liscia.

Versare la glassa fusa uniformemente sopra i quadrotti al pistacchio raffreddati. Spolverare immediatamente con i mirtilli rossi tritati e altri pistacchi tritati per guarnire. Lasciare rapprendere la glassa prima di tagliare a quadrotti e servire.
